findings.blocker: Sub-step (a) IsCompatible/cocycle (L565) is blocked on the hypothesis `hf`. `hf i j` is an HEq between gi := (pullback (resLE (𝟙 X)(U i)(U i⊓U j))).map (f i) and gj := the analogous j-image. Consuming an HEq of morphisms into an Eq needs the source/target OBJECT equalities, but each object is a SHEAFIFICATION of a pullback presheaf and they are only ISOMORPHIC, not propositionally equal: rfl fails, exact? fails, congr 1 exposes the false residual goal (↑U i).Modules = (↑U j).Modules (different source categories), and no SheafOfModules/PresheafOfModules HEq-extensionality exists (leansearch returns only the homogeneous Hom.ext). Subsingleton.elim only collapses the thin Opens route-difference, not the non-subsingleton Ab hom-set agreement of f i vs f j. `hf` (as phrased) is the only link and is not consumable — arguably not even satisfiable by a caller, who would hit the same object-equality wall. NOTE: `homOfLocalCompat` is NOT in archon-protected.yaml; the prover treated it as frozen but re-signing hf is a planner/blueprint/refactor decision, not a user-only block.
